notes: Works out of the box without any noticeable graphical or audio hiccups, albeit with abysmal performance until turning the graphics detail as low as possible. There are however major toothing issues with mouse support: occasionally it will become unusable in menus, but worse, if two stages are played in a row using M+KB (including the one-time intro stage), mouse aim will spaz out and become completely unusable. Controller support is unaffected. Playing through multiple stages with the controller does not seem to break mouse aim. It could be tied to starting or ending a stage with the controller, or even just using the controller briefly to reset the mouse aim. Further testing required. If you don't have a controller handy, YOU MUST restart the game between each stage to not have your run ended by technical difficulties.
title: Void Bastards

notes: Works flawlessly on 4.2-5. Sometimes there's an input issue with the mouse -- specifically it will get hyper-sensitive -- but changing from full screen to windowed seems to fix that (and then you can go back to full screen). I don't think this is an issue with Void Bastards specifically as i've seen some input lag and related quirks in other games. Note: for some reason enabling Caps Lock prevents you from moving the mouse in game, which is kind of odd
title: Void Bastards

notes: Requires DX11. Awful performance, playable at very low resolutions with settings on minimum. Fullscreen mode is crashy. Very minor graphical glitches. Otherwise technically playable.
title: Void Bastards

notes: Performance is vastly improved by using WINED3D11, at the tradeoff of inducing some Z-fighting in distant shadows. Mouse aim still tends to spaz out as a result of using M+KB at some indistinct point in the menus between levels, or from alt-tabbing. It can be fixed by toggling to windowed mode and back again while ingame (not while in the pause menu), but this is also inconsistent and can require multiple tries. TL;DR: Use a controller for all your menuing between stages to avoid mouse issues.
title: Void Bastards

notes: Mouse behave like a game controller with insane sensibility in fullscreen. No problem in windowed but if you go windowed on gnome the huge title bar just hide important bottom part of the screen. I choosed to go with a lower vertical resolution but on a widescreen the 16/9 like display is just too anoying
title: Void Bastards

notes: Works fine out of the box. I have to open the Application Launcher after every level to fix mouse movement.
title: Void Bastards

notes: Game is totally playable, No Performance Issues noticed so far. The only thing that bothers me is the mouse. in menues it can stuck or ingame it gets Hyper sensitivity. This gets fixed for me on alt-tabing to the desktop and back to the game. no deal breaker for me.
title: Void Bastards

notes: The game runs out of the box and I get consistent 100+ FPS on my Radeon RX 580 8GB at 2560 x 1080 resolution, max settings, FOV @ 75. VRAM usage is around 3GB. The only problem is when in-game and you tried to access menu (press Esc), mouse cursor will not move. Just press Alt+Tab to make the mouse work in menus and comic book cutscenes. However, when you return to in-game, mouse will wildly move, just press Alt+Tab again. Total play time of 27 minutes, no crashes, no graphical glitches. Another game showcasing Steam Play is awesome.
title: Void Bastards

notes:
Game runs "ok" for the most part, good performance with proton, better with D3D11. After every mission the mouse goes absolutely nuts, camera starts to spin, you can't click on anything, etc. These issues are fixed with alt+tab sometimes, other times require launching the game again which, since it's a roguelite, means killing the current character.

I don't understand the other reports giving the game a "platinum" rate, the mouse issue is pretty significant. Platinum means it runs perfectly.
title: Void Bastards

notes: Works out of the box except requiring alt+tab to reset mouse movement at the start of each stage (costing a few seconds time) and additionally at the end of each stage.  Once alt+tab is done the game runs normally.
title: Void Bastards

notes:
The game ran fine without the Wine D3d11 tweak but occasionally after playing through one or two areas the next one you'd enter shunted the fps down to  around 10. After enabling the tweak however it ran smooth as butter around 100-120 fps. 

However after 20~ish hours now the problem has come back, even with D3d11 enabled. Very unsure about what is causing it but this happens even after a full reboot with nothing else running in the background.
title: Void Bastards

notes: Runs great except for the mouse issue. I set it to window mode and the mouse works fine though. The game plays perfectly at fullscreen with a gamepad but it makes the game much harder.
